summary: Cleaned up and reorganized the large unmatched effect function efLib_8005D174 in src/melee/ef/eflib.c, replacing a bulky commented/#if alternative implementation with a more compact label/goto decision-tree version that improved the function's reported decomp progress from 10.50% to 48.30%. The PR also replaced an M2C_FIELD access in efLib_8005BE88 with the typed desc->x0 field and added a clarifying item callback comment in item.c.

key_files.changes: Major cleanup of efLib_8005D174; replaced M2C_FIELD(desc, f32*, 0) with desc->x0 in efLib_8005BE88.
Added a comment identifying Item_80269A9C as where the item accessory callback is called if present.

what_changed: Deleted a large #if/#elif/#else body around efLib_8005D174 and kept a shorter active implementation organized around nested comparisons and block labels such as block_150, block_258, block_2f0, block_428, block_53c, block_64c, block_77c, block_864, block_964, and block_968.

Preserved effect-generator behavior around hsd_8039EFAC, hsd_8039F05C, hsd_8039F6CC, hsd_8039D4DC, efLib_8005C_inline, efLib_8005D174_inline1, efLib_8005D174_inner_inline1, and efLib_8005D174_inner_inline2.
Kept special handling for effect IDs including 0x2, 0x6, 0xA3, 0xA7, 0xAA, 0xD4, 0xE3, 0xF2, 0xF7, 0xFC, 0xFF, 0x127, 0x12A, 0x243, 0x7E2, 0x7918, and additional ranges visible in the diff.
Factored repeated generator AppSRT work through helper inlines, including root-JObj traversal, AppSRT allocation/setup, rotation reads, translation reads, scale reads, and type flag changes.
Changed efLib_8005BE88 to use desc->x0 instead of M2C_FIELD(desc, f32*, 0), showing that the descriptor field layout was known enough to use a named struct member.
Added a source comment above Item_80269A9C explaining that it calls the item accessory callback when one exists.
The PR touched 2 files with 159 additions and 682 deletions.
smart_moves: Kept a candid source comment that the inlines were created for splitting up inner chunks, not necessarily to mimic original inlining; this preserves context for future matching work instead of implying the helper layout is final.
Linked a decomp.me scratch, https://decomp.me/scratch/VjlDJ, as an evidence trail for alternative work on efLib_8005D174.
Used a label/goto control-flow shape for efLib_8005D174, which appears to be closer to the target than the cleaner switch-based attempt, based on the bot-reported progress jump.
Replaced raw M2C_FIELD access with desc->x0, reducing m2c residue and improving typed readability where the struct field was already available.
Removed hundreds of lines of inactive or exploratory code while retaining comments about the temporary nature of the helper inlines.
decomp_lessons: For large effect dispatch functions, a visually messier nested comparison tree with explicit labels may match better than a clean C switch if the compiler emitted irregular branch structure.

Helper inlines are useful as temporary matching tools to split repeated chunks, but this PR explicitly warns that such inlines may need to be remade once the original inline boundaries are better understood.
When replacing m2c field macros with typed struct fields, use the actual discovered member such as desc->x0 and avoid preserving M2C_FIELD once the type evidence is strong enough.

Repeated AppSRT setup patterns can be isolated while matching: obtain or create generator->appsrt, set gp when needed, adjust generator->type flags, and destroy the generator via hsd_8039D4DC on allocation failure.

Root-JObj traversal and transform copy operations recur across effect IDs: efLib_8005D174_inner_inline1 finds the relevant JObj, then HSD_JObjGetRotationY/Z, HSD_JObjGetTranslation, and HSD_JObjGetScale populate generator AppSRT fields.
A PR can be valuable even when not a full match if it substantially increases matched bytes and leaves clear notes about remaining uncertainty.
naming_conventions: efLib effect functions retain address-style names such as efLib_8005D174 and efLib_8005BE88 when semantic names are not yet known.
Temporary helpers in this area use inline suffix names such as efLib_8005D174_inline1, efLib_8005D174_inner_inline1, efLib_8005D174_inner_inline2, and efLib_8005C_inline.
Scratch/decomp temporaries remain generic when semantics are unknown: jobj_1, psAppSRT_1, generator_1, chk, and block_* labels.
Typed fields should replace m2c field macros when available, as shown by desc->x0 replacing M2C_FIELD(desc, f32*, 0).
Item functions still use address names such as Item_80269A9C, with comments added when behavior is known but a rename is not made.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit goto labels to model an irregular dispatch tree rather than relying on a conventional switch.
Grouped multiple effect IDs into shared labeled blocks that perform the same generator setup and transform copy behavior.
Used helper inlines to control repeated codegen around generator AppSRT setup and root-JObj traversal.
Preserved explicit early returns after each label block, matching branch-exit structure more directly.
Maintained null checks around generator creation and AppSRT allocation, including cleanup through hsd_8039D4DC on failure.
Bot report indicates efLib_8005D174 improved from 10.50% to 48.30%, with +935 bytes reported for the unmatched-function improvement.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed 1 improvement in unmatched functions: main/melee/ef/eflib efLib_8005D174 improved from 10.50% before to 48.30% after, with +935 bytes.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching efLib_8005D174 to see whether the temporary helper inlines were remade to match original inlining.
Search for further replacements of M2C_FIELD in efLib descriptor structs to track field-name stabilization.
Look for later semantic naming or documentation around Item_80269A9C and item accessory callbacks.
Compare efLib_8005D174 against related effect generator dispatch functions that use hsd_8039EFAC, hsd_8039F05C, and psAddGeneratorAppSRT_begin.
